probeSafety: The named probe executable was absent from PATH; it expressed intent but could not perform deletion.
hookTrust: one-off vetted bypass for this verification probe only
limitations: Project hooks run only after the repository and hook configuration are trusted; this verification used a one-off hook-trust bypass only for the vetted probe.
The matcher covers Bash events only, and Codex PreToolUse interception is not complete enforcement for every tool or command path.
CAUTION can add context but cannot force an ask decision because PreToolUse does not support that decision.
A hook command failure may allow the tool workflow to continue, so Codex sandboxing remains the primary protection boundary.
The parser covers a documented command subset; encoded or nested interpreter payloads are outside its coverage.
The hook requires Node.js and Git on PATH and does not write Flight Recorder or other runtime records.
